etymology.description: 㐅 (rock/mud/stone/bamboo) + 凵 (hole) -> a hole filled with something -> fierce
etymology.interpretation: The character depicts a hole, possibly filled with a substance like rock or mud, symbolizing something dangerous or threatening. This imagery reflects the idea of 'fierce' as something ominous or perilous emerging from the ground.

examples.chinese: 他的眼神很凶(他的眼神很兇)。
这只狗看起来很凶(這隻狗看起來很兇)，但是其实很友好(但是其實很友好)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè zhī gǒu kàn qǐlái hěn xiōng, dànshì qíshí hěn yǒuhǎo.
examples.english: This dog looks fierce, but it is actually very friendly.

examples.chinese: 他对待敌人非常凶(他對待敵人非常兇)，毫不留情(毫不留情)。
examples.pinyin: Tā duì dài dírén fēicháng xiōng, háobù liúqíng.
examples.english: He is very fierce towards his enemies, showing no mercy.
